comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, this child as one of two consumers]

4-bucket map of parent criteria vs children (roster: 908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer):
- SC1 (primitive consumed by BOTH parent+link, no Jira logic in gate): FULLY (joint) — 908c consumes gate for links, ba2c for parent.
- AC1 (reducer maintains/folds managed_refs, never reduced by UNLINK): STRUCTURAL — primitive-build is parent's own in-scope work; consumers only read it.
- AC2 (survives compact_ticket; compact-then-reconcile test): COVERED behaviorally — 908c AC5 'survives a compaction boundary' + ba2c 'across a compaction boundary'; restore mechanism is parent-side.
- AC3 (SNAPSHOT byte-parity / deterministic serialization): STRUCTURAL — parent-owned serialization.
- AC4 (shared should_propagate_removal consumed by BOTH outbound paths): FULLY (joint) — 908c link path + ba2c parent path.
- AC5 (reclamation prunes converged refs + test): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope explicitly lists 'reclamation' as primitive's own deliverable, not a consumer.
- AC6 (docs/ template for future providers): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope explicitly lists 'docs establishing the reusable pattern'.

Result: no uncovered parent criterion attributable to children. Parent is a primitive-building story that carves reducer/byte-parity/reclamation/docs into its OWN in-scope work and tracks only the two CONSUMERS as children; both consumer halves are covered (links here, parent in ba2c). Clean for G3.

comments.body: [G3 child-coverage, this child as link-consumer half of parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6] Re-verified against whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer). 4-bucket: SC1/AC4 (shared gate consumed by BOTH paths) jointly FULLY covered — 908c covers the link outbound path (`_diff_links` managed-ref-gated REMOVE), ba2c covers the parent CLEAR path; AC2 (survives compaction) behaviorally covered by 908c AC5 'survives a compaction boundary' + ba2c 'across a compaction boundary'. AC1/AC3/AC5/AC6 (reducer fold, byte-parity, reclamation, docs) are STRUCTURAL — parent Scope explicitly retains these as its OWN in-scope primitive-build deliverables ("the reducer field + compaction preservation + byte-parity; the shared gate helper; reclamation; docs"), delegating only the two CONSUMERS to children. No uncovered parent criterion attributable to children. Clean for G3.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (908c link-consumer) vs whole roster (908c + ba2c parent-consumer)]

Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes; all clean (high-confidence, benign-reading filter applied):

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned shared state, declared in parent What/Scope. Both children only READ the gate/set; the reducer fold + serialization is parent in-scope work. No hidden/undeclared shared mutation. Verified `managed_refs` does not yet exist in tree (parent builds it). CLEAN.

2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent => REMOVE iff in managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, NO inbound-remove dual. 908c ("NO inbound-remove dual ... consistent with parent") matches ba2c (CLEAR only when in managed_refs, else ADOPT) and parent's `should_propagate_removal` definition. CLEAN.

3. Dependency gap — 908c logically depends on the parent primitive/gate existing; the parent-child hierarchy (b4d4 is the parent ticket building it) supplies that ordering, and parent Scope explicitly tracks the two CONSUMERS as children. No undeclared gap. CLEAN.

4. Scope overlap — 908c touches the LINK path (`_diff_links`, new acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE, inbound link-ADD suppression); sibling ba2c touches the PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`/emit_detach_clear). Disjoint code paths. Shared gate is common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic. Verified root-cause cites against code: outbound `_diff_links` ADD-only (outbound_differ.py:1002-1040), inbound `_diff_links_inbound` ADD-only (inbound_differ.py:440-514) — accurate. CLEAN.

5. Ordering violation — 908c and ba2c are independent consumers (links vs parent); no inter-child ordering dependency; both gated behind the parent primitive. CLEAN.

6. Consumer impact — both children add same-pass suppression in the inbound differ, but on DISJOINT ref kinds (908c: link-ADD echo; ba2c: parent adoption). No cross-interference. CLEAN.

7. Residual references — 908c references parent alias "safe-luge-nog"; confirmed b4d4's alias IS safe-luge-nog. No stale/dangling reference. CLEAN.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).

(Note for author, NON-G4 — single-child internal underspecification, not a cross-child contradiction: the Jira "Blocks" type collapses both `blocks` and `depends_on` logical kinds direction-agnostically (inbound_differ.py:497-502; outbound dedup adds both inward/outward keys at outbound_differ.py:995-998), while `managed_refs` keys on direction-distinguished `(kind, target)`. The link-REMOVE gate-lookup against a direction-collapsed Jira link vs direction-keyed managed_refs is unspecified. Flagged here only as context; it is a G2-class within-child gap, not a G4 finding.)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (908c link-consumer) vs whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer)]

4-bucket audit of parent SC/AC vs the children, with every flagged absence cross-checked against the WHOLE roster:

- SC1 (provider-agnostic primitive consumed by BOTH parent + link removal, no Jira logic in gate): FULLY (joint) — 908c consumes the gate on the LINK path (`_diff_links` managed-ref-gated REMOVE; never-managed Jira link adopted inbound); ba2c consumes it on the PARENT CLEAR path. Both halves present.
- AC1 (reducer maintains/folds managed_refs: monotonic, sorted, never reduced by UNLINK): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope explicitly retains "the reducer managed_refs field + compaction preservation + byte-parity" as its OWN in-scope primitive-build work; consumers only READ the set. Not delegated to children, so not a child-attributable gap.
- AC2 (absent managed_refs → seed from deps+parent_id; pre-feature-compacted test): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope explicitly retains "migration-seeding." Seeding/its test are parent-owned.
- AC3 (managed_refs survives compact_ticket; compact-then-reconcile test): COVERED behaviorally on the consumer paths — 908c AC5 "survives a compaction boundary" + ba2c "across a compaction boundary"; the restore mechanism (process_snapshot) is parent-side.
- AC4 (SNAPSHOT byte-parity / deterministic serialization): STRUCTURAL — parent-owned serialization, in parent Scope.
- AC5 (shared should_propagate_removal gate exists, provider-agnostic, defaults empty→False, unit-tested REMOVE-vs-ADOPT): gate existence + its OWN unit tests are STRUCTURAL (parent Scope: "the shared provider-agnostic gate helper (with its own unit tests)"); the REMOVE-vs-ADOPT DECISION is additionally exercised jointly through the consumers (908c link path, ba2c parent path). Covered.
- AC6 (operator-facing regression: unlink a managed ref, two reconcile passes, stays removed — churn observably fixed): COVERED (joint) — 908c AC5 "local-unlink of a managed link emits an outbound remove and is not re-added inbound" is exactly the churn-fixed regression for the LINK kind; ba2c covers the PARENT kind (human-set parent adopted; managed parent CLEAR converges). No roster gap.
- AC7 (docs/ documents the pattern + future reclamation hook as template): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope explicitly retains "docs establishing the reusable pattern."

Absence cross-check: every parent criterion is either (a) jointly covered by the two named CONSUMERS (SC1, AC4 decision, AC3 durability, AC6 regression) or (b) parent-owned in-scope primitive-build work the parent's Scope deliberately retains rather than delegating (AC1, AC2, byte-parity, gate unit-tests, docs). This container is a primitive-building story that carves reducer/seeding/byte-parity/gate-helper/docs into its OWN scope and tracks only the two consumer integrations as children. This child (link consumer) fully covers its half; sibling ba2c covers the parent half. No uncovered parent criterion is attributable to the children.

Result: G3 CLEAN (empty findings).

comments.body:
[G4 child-consistency re-audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (908c link-consumer) vs whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer)]

Re-checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes (high-confidence, benign-reading filter applied); all CLEAN. Verified against code:

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned shared state (parent What/Scope). Confirmed via search: `managed_refs` has zero matches in the tree → parent builds it; both children only READ the gate/set. No hidden/undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.

2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent ⇒ REMOVE/CLEAR iff ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, NO inbound-remove dual. 908c ("NO inbound-remove dual … consistent with parent") matches ba2c (CLEAR only when ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT) and parent's `should_propagate_removal`. CLEAN.

3. Dependency gap — 908c logically depends on the parent primitive/gate; both children carry parent_id b4d4 (the primitive builder), supplying ordering. Parent Scope explicitly tracks the two CONSUMERS as children. CLEAN.

4. Scope overlap — 908c = LINK path (`_diff_links`, new acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE, inbound link-ADD suppression); ba2c = PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`/`emit_detach_clear`). Disjoint code paths. Root-cause cites verified accurate: outbound `_diff_links` ADD-only ("No REMOVE mutations are emitted", outbound_differ.py:1020-1021); inbound `_diff_links_inbound` ADD-only ("No REMOVE mutations", inbound_differ.py:463-464). Shared gate is common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic. CLEAN.

5. Ordering violation — 908c and ba2c are independent consumers (links vs parent); no inter-child ordering dependency; both gated behind the parent primitive. CLEAN.

6. Consumer impact — both children extend same-pass inbound suppression, but on DISJOINT keys: 908c uses the link-add echo set `link_add_keys` (inbound_differ.py:599-601,619-623), ba2c uses the scalar `fields`/`parent`→`parent_id` path (_OUTBOUND_TO_INBOUND_FIELD, inbound_differ.py:578-581,633-644). No cross-interference. CLEAN.

7. Residual references — 908c references parent alias "safe-luge-nog"; confirmed b4d4's alias IS safe-luge-nog. No stale/dangling reference. CLEAN.

Re-affirming the prior NON-G4 note (within-child / G2-class, NOT a cross-child contradiction): the Jira "Blocks" type collapses both `blocks` and `depends_on` logical kinds direction-agnostically (inbound_differ.py:497-502; outbound `_existing_jira_links` adds both inward/outward keys, outbound_differ.py:995-998), while parent-defined `managed_refs` keys on direction-distinguished `(kind, target)`. 908c's link-REMOVE gate lookup against a direction-collapsed Jira "Blocks" link cannot deterministically reconstruct whether to probe `(blocks, target)` or `(depends_on, target)` — an underspecification INTERNAL to 908c's gate consumption. It does NOT contradict sibling ba2c (parent ref is single-direction `(parent, target)`, unambiguous), so it is a within-child gap, not a cross-child interaction defect. Confirmed G2-class, out of scope for G4.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).

7 cross-child interaction modes, high-confidence + benign-reading filter, verified against code:

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are explicitly parent-owned (parent What/Scope); both children only READ. Reducer fold + serialization is parent in-scope work. No undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.
2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent ⇒ REMOVE/CLEAR iff ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, NO inbound-remove dual. 908c ("NO inbound-remove dual … consistent with parent") aligns with ba2c and parent. CLEAN.
3. Dependency gap — 908c depends on the parent primitive/gate; parent-child hierarchy (both children parent_id b4d4) supplies ordering; parent Scope tracks the two consumers. CLEAN.
4. Scope overlap — 908c = LINK path (`_diff_links`, acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE, inbound link-ADD suppression); ba2c = PARENT-CLEAR path. Disjoint code paths. Root-cause cites verified: outbound `_diff_links` ADD-only ("No REMOVE mutations are emitted", outbound_differ.py:1020); shared gate is common CONSUMPTION not duplicated logic. CLEAN.
5. Ordering violation — independent consumers; no inter-child ordering dependency. CLEAN.
6. Consumer impact — both extend same-pass inbound suppression on DISJOINT keys (908c link-add echo vs ba2c parent/scalar adoption). No cross-interference. CLEAN.
7. Residual references — parent alias "safe-luge-nog" confirmed = b4d4. CLEAN.

NON-G4 note (within-child / G2-class, reaffirmed): Jira "Blocks" collapses both `blocks` and `depends_on` direction-agnostically in `_existing_jira_links` ((type_name,key), outbound_differ.py:995-998), while parent `managed_refs` keys on direction-distinguished `(kind,target)`. 908c's link-REMOVE gate lookup must recover direction from the Jira link's inward/outward side (as inbound already does, inbound_differ.py:492-502) before probing `(blocks,target)` vs `(depends_on,target)`. This is resolvable inside 908c's own implementation and does NOT contradict parent normalization or sibling ba2c (single-direction `(parent,target)`); a within-child underspecification, not a cross-child interaction defect.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Completion verification — FAIL

Verified each AC against the repository implementation. Four of five ACs are substantively met; one has a concrete discrepancy against the specified contract.

---

### AC1 — `_diff_links` managed-ref-gated REMOVE; never-managed link not removed
**MET.**
`outbound_differ.py:1113-1175` (`_diff_link_removals`) implements the gate: calls `should_propagate_removal(relation, local_target, ticket)` for each Jira link of a mapped type absent locally, emitting `{"action":"remove",...}` only when the ref is in `managed_refs`, and skipping unmanaged links entirely. Direction disambiguation (inward → `base_relation`; outward → `depends_on` when base is `blocks`) mirrors the inbound differ.
Tests: `test_managed_link_absent_locally_emits_remove` and `test_unmanaged_jira_link_is_not_removed` in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_removal_sync.py:102-129`.

---

### AC2 — New acli delete-relationship method; 404-on-gone treated as success; unit-tested
**NOT MET (method name/signature mismatch).**
The ticket specifies `delete_relationship(from_key, to_key, link_type)`. The codebase contains no method with that name anywhere (`grep delete_relationship` → zero matches). The implementation delivers `delete_issue_link(link_id: str)` in `acli_graph.py:587-607` (ID-based, not key-based). The functional capability (removing a Jira issue link, idempotent on CalledProcessError/404, unit-tested via `test_delete_issue_link_dispatches_to_run`) is present, but the specified API contract — caller passes `(from_key, to_key, link_type)` and the method resolves the link ID — is not implemented. Instead, link-ID resolution is pushed to `batch_dispatch._find_link_id` (`batch_dispatch.py:67-87`). The 404-on-gone handling is also at the batch_dispatch caller level (`batch_dispatch.py:652-660`), not inside the acli method itself.

---

### AC3 — `batch_dispatch.update_one` applies link REMOVE, HTTP errors non-fatal
**MET.**
`batch_dispatch.py:621-666`: the REMOVE branch probes `get_issue_links`, resolves the link ID via `_find_link_id`, calls `client.delete_issue_link(link_id)`. `CalledProcessError` (ACLI failure, covers 404/409) is caught and treated as idempotent success (`_links_applied += 1` without raising, `batch_dispatch.py:652-660`). Generic `Exception` is also caught non-fatally (`batch_dispatch.py:661-666`).
Tests: `test_update_one_applies_link_remove` and `test_update_one_tolerates_acli_delete_failure` in `test_link_removal_sync.py:204-239`.

---

### AC4 — Same-pass suppression drops the inbound link-ADD echo of an outbound link REMOVE
**MET.**
`inbound_differ.py:627-631`: `_build_outbound_context` populates `entry["link_remove_keys"]` for outbound `action=="remove"` links. `inbound_differ.py:751-766`: `_suppress_keys = ob_entry["link_add_keys"] | ob_entry["link_remove_keys"]`; any inbound link-ADD whose resolved target key is in that union is dropped (suppression_count incremented).
Test: `test_outbound_remove_suppresses_inbound_link_readd` in `test_link_removal_sync.py:147-169` — demonstrates the control case (bare inbound re-adds the link) and the coordinated case (suppressed).

---

### AC5 — Regression tests (RED→GREEN, mutation-checked): local-unlink of managed link; never-managed adopted; survives compaction
**MET (tests exist, no skip/xfail).**
- "local-unlink of a managed link emits an outbound remove and is not re-added inbound": covered end-to-end by `test_outbound_remove_suppresses_inbound_link_readd` and the three outbound-remove unit tests in `test_link_removal_sync.py`.
- "a never-managed Jira link is adopted not removed": `test_unmanaged_jira_link_is_not_removed`.
- "survives a compaction boundary": `tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py:129-148` (`test_managed_refs_survive_compaction_roundtrip`) verifies that `managed_refs` round-trips through a SNAPSHOT (compact simulation) unchanged, so a removal performed pre-compaction still propagates post-compaction.
- "mutation-checked" is a process assertion not verifiable from the repository; no contrary signal (no skip/xfail).

---

### Overall verdict: FAIL
AC2 is not met: the specified acli method `delete_relationship(from_key, to_key, link_type)` does not exist. The implementation substitutes `delete_issue_link(link_id)` with link-ID resolution in `batch_dispatch._find_link_id`. The functional goal is achieved but the AC contract (method name and signature) is not satisfied.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Two tickets A, B bound to Jira with a `blocks`/`relates_to`/`depends_on` link present BOTH locally and as a Jira issuelink.
2. Remove the link locally: `rebar unlink A B`.
3. Run a reconcile pass.

**Expected:** the Jira issuelink is removed to match local; the unlink sticks.
**Actual:** the local unlink is silently reverted every pass (outbound `_diff_links` is ADD-only -> Jira keeps the link -> inbound `_diff_links_inbound` re-adds it locally).

## Root Cause

Same asymmetric-clear class as parent-detach: both link differs are ADD-only (`outbound_differ.py:983-1040`, `inbound_differ.py:440-514`); the applier has no remove-link path (`batch_dispatch.py:554-595`) and acli has no delete-link method.

## Design (consumes the shared managed-ref gate from parent story safe-luge-nog)

Make link removal LOCAL-AUTHORITATIVE via the shared, compaction-surviving managed-ref provenance gate:
- Outbound `_diff_links` emits a REMOVE for a Jira link of a mapped type, absent locally, ONLY when the ref is in `managed_refs` (deliberate unlink) — a never-managed Jira link is left for inbound ADOPT, never clobbered.
- New acli `delete_relationship(from_key, to_key, link_type)` (treat 404-on-already-gone as success).
- `batch_dispatch.update_one` routes the REMOVE through it, guarding HTTP errors non-fatally.
- Same-pass suppression: when outbound emits a link REMOVE for to_key, suppress the inbound link-ADD for that target (remove-wins) so local wins.
- NO inbound-remove dual (keeps links local-authoritative, consistent with parent).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_diff_links` emits a managed-ref-gated REMOVE for a Jira link absent locally; a never-managed Jira link emits NO remove (adopted inbound instead).
- [ ] New acli delete-relationship method removes a Jira issuelink; 404-on-gone treated as success; unit-tested.
- [ ] `batch_dispatch.update_one` applies the link REMOVE, HTTP errors non-fatal.
- [ ] Same-pass suppression drops the inbound link-ADD echo of an outbound link REMOVE.
- [ ] Regression tests (RED->GREEN, mutation-checked): local-unlink of a managed link emits an outbound remove and is not re-added inbound; a never-managed Jira link is adopted not removed; survives a compaction boundary.
compiled_state.title: Issue-link/dep REMOVAL churn: same asymmetric-clear pattern as parent-detach (both differs ADD-only)
